page: Microsoft Azure F4als_v7 is a compute-optimized virtual machine belonging to the Falsv7 server family. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD processor with an x86_64 architecture. The instance is configured with 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core.

This server type runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It does not include local storage, meaning all storage needs must be met via network-attached options. However, the instance is designated as Premium Storage capable, allowing integration with high-performance external storage volumes. It does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Due to its lower memory-to-CPU ratio, the F4als_v7 offers a cost-efficient profile for workloads that require dedicated compute resources without the overhead of large memory allocations. This hardware configuration is suitable for compute-bound tasks, batch processing, web servers, and lightweight application hosting where 2.0 GB of RAM per core is sufficient.
description: Microsoft Azure F4als_v7 is an x86_64 compute-optimized virtual machine from the Falsv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD processor and is equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory, providing a ratio of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance does not include local storage but is Premium Storage capable, allowing connection to high-performance external storage volumes. It also does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses. This resource profile is designed for compute-bound workloads that do not require large memory footprints, offering a cost-efficient option for dedicated CPU allocation. Suitable workloads include batch processing, web serving, and lightweight application hosting.
